CheerpX
=======

x86 Linux in WebAssembly. Used by peerd-engine as the sandboxed VM
runtime for vm_boot / vm_import / vm_write_file.

Upstream
--------
Project:   https://leaningtech.com/cheerpx/
Docs:      https://cheerpx.io/docs
CDN base:  https://cxrtnc.leaningtech.com/<version>/
Disk:      wss://disks.webvm.io/debian_large_20230522_5044875331.ext2
           (CheerpX's stock Debian image, streaming)

Version pinned to: 1.2.8

cx.esm.js sha256: 87b0809f469286a11efdec01dc5873205d50fd0dea710dd6eaa72434de760899

Vendoring model
---------------
MV3 extension_pages CSP forbids BOTH `script-src https://<external>`
AND `blob:` (we tried both — Chrome rejects the manifest in either
case). The only legitimate path is to commit the runtime bytes
locally. `scripts/vendor-cheerpx.sh` is the reproducible step:

  1. Edit CHEERPX_VERSION in the script.
  2. Run it from the repo root. It curls the pinned files into
     this directory and prints the sha of the entry file.
  3. Update this SOURCE.txt with the new version + sha.
  4. Commit.

Vendored files
--------------
  cx.esm.js                    — entry; thin wrapper around cx_esm.js
  cx_esm.js                    — main bundle
  cxcore.js / cxcore.wasm      — Wasm core + JS shim
  cxcore-no-return-call.{js,wasm} — variant for browsers without
                                    WebAssembly return_call support
  cxbridge.js                  — host bridge
  cheerpOS.js                  — minimal OS layer
  workerclock.js               — worker-side clock for emulated time
  tun/direct.js                — network: direct (passthrough)
  tun/tailscale_tun_auto.js    — network: tailscale (unused in V1
                                  since VM is sandboxed)

License
-------
Non-commercial free tier. peerd V1 use confirmed in scope with
Leaning Tech (2026). Commercial peerd deployments will need a
separate license — flag before that ship.

CSP impact
----------
Only connect-src is relaxed for the disk-image stream (we still need
that — the image isn't vendored, it streams lazily over WebSocket):

  connect-src 'self' https://disks.webvm.io wss://disks.webvm.io

script-src stays 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval' — the strictest MV3
allows. No external script origins, no blob:.

Updating
--------
1. Bump CHEERPX_VERSION in scripts/vendor-cheerpx.sh.
2. Run the script.
3. Update this file's version + sha lines.
4. Smoke test in the side panel: `vm_boot("uname -a")`.
5. If the API has shifted, cheerpx-adapter.js is the only file
   that needs updating.
